name: backend-ai-account-manager

metadata.name: backend.ai-account-manager
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 or later (LGPLv3+)

documentation:
# Backend.AI Account Manager

Backend.AI Account Manager is a daemon that manages user accounts, including Backend.AI 's first-party service.

functions.name: ai.backend.account_manager.models.alembic.env.run_migrations_offline
documentation:
Run migrations in 'offline' mode.

This configures the context with just a URL
and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.

Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
script output.
other values from the config, defined by the needs of env.py,
can be acquired:
my_important_option = config.get_main_option("my_important_option")
... etc.

functions.name: ai.backend.account_manager.models.alembic.env.run_async_migrations
documentation:
In this scenario we need to create an Engine
and associate a connection with the context.

functions.name: ai.backend.account_manager.models.alembic.env.run_migrations_online
documentation: Run migrations in 'online' mode.

methods.name: ai.backend.account_manager.models.utils.ExtendedAsyncSAEngine.execute_with_txn_retry
documentation:
Execute DB related function by retrying transaction in a given connection.

The transaction retry resolves Postgres's Serialization error.
Reference: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/mvcc-serialization-failure-handling.html
A subclass to add a few more convenience methods to the SQLAlchemy's async engine.

Uses PostgreSQL's UUID type, otherwise uses CHAR(16) storing as raw bytes.

modules.name: ai.backend.account_manager.exceptions
documentation:
This module defines a series of Backend.AI-specific errors based on HTTP Error
classes from aiohttp.
Raising a BackendError is automatically mapped to a corresponding HTTP error
response with RFC7807-style JSON-encoded description in its response body.

In the client side, you should use "type" field in the body to distinguish
canonical error types beacuse "title" field may change due to localization and
future UX improvements.

classes.name: ai.backend.account_manager.exceptions.BackendError
documentation:
An RFC-7807 error class as a drop-in replacement of the original
aiohttp.web.HTTPError subclasses.

classes.name: ai.backend.account_manager.plugin.WebappPlugin
documentation:
Webapp plugins should create a valid aiohttp.web.Application instance. The returned app
instance will be a subapp of the root app defined by the account manager.

The init/cleanup methods of the plugin are ignored and the account manager uses the standard aiohttp's
application lifecycle handlers attached to the returned app instance.

params.name:
Field(
        description="Address to access the etcd.",
        examples=[HostPortPair(host="127.0.0.1", port=8121)],
    )

Field(
        description=f"DB type. One of {[_type.value for _type in DBType]}. Default is 'postgresql'.",
        default=DBType.POSTGRESQL,
    )

Field(
        description=f"Transaction isolation level. One of {[val.value for val in TransactionIsolationLevel]}. Default is 'SERIALIZABLE'.",
        default=TransactionIsolationLevel.SERIALIZABLE,
    )

Field(
        description="Address to access the database.",
        examples=[HostPortPair(host="127.0.0.1", port=8001)],
    )

Field(
        description=f"The connection pool's initial size. Default is {_default_db_pool_size}.",
        default=_default_db_pool_size,
    )

Field(
        description=f"This setting causes the pool to recycle connections after the given number of seconds has passed. Default is {_default_pool_recycle}, which means infinite.",
        default=_default_pool_recycle,
    )

Field(
        description="This setting eliminates DB error due to stale pooled connections by ping at the start of each connection pool checkout. Default is 'false'.",
        default=False,
    )

Field(
        description=f"The maximum allowed overflow of the connection pool. Default is {_default_max_overflow}.",
        default=_default_max_overflow,
    )

Field(
        description="Address of account-manager service.",
        examples=[HostPortPair(host="127.0.0.1", port=8099)],
    )

Field(
        default=Path("/tmp/backend.ai/ipc"),
        description="Directory to store temporary UNIX sockets.",
    )
Field(default=EventLoopType.ASYNCIO, description="Type of event loop to use.")

Field(
        default=Path(os.devnull),
        description="Place to store process PID.",
        examples=["/tmp/backend.ai/account_manager.pid"],
    )
Field(description="Use TLS to communicate. Default is false.", default=False)
Field(description="SSL Certificate file path. Default is null.", default=None)
Field(description="SSL Private key file path. Default is null.", default=False)

Field(
        description=f"Number of forked workers. Default is {_default_num_worker}.",
        default=_default_num_worker,
    )
Field(description="Plugins to allow. Default is null, which skips allowing.", default=None)
Field(description="Plugins to block. Default is null, which skips blocking.", default=None)
